data.title: Bain Capital to acquire 9.9% stake in Lincoln Financial for $825 million
data.description:
• Lincoln Financial (NYSE:LNC) Wednesday announced an $825 million strategic growth investment from Bain Capital for a 9.9% stake in the company. 
  • LNC will sell approximately 18.8 million shares of its common stock for $44.00 per share, based on a 25% premium to the 30-day volume-weighted average price as of April 8, 2025.
  • Under the terms of the agreement, Bain Capital (NYSE:BCSF) has agreed to certain limitations and restrictions on its ability to divest its ownership stake.
  • The partnership will enable Lincoln to accelerate its strategy, and provide growth capital to be deployed in areas of strategic focus, the company said.

data.title: Trump Media TV streaming apps roll out in Canada and Mexico
data.description:
• Trump Media and Technology Group (NASDAQ:DJT) has announced the launch of its Truth+ TV streaming app and connected TV apps in Canada and Mexico.
  • The Truth+ mobile app is now available throughout Canada and Mexico in the Apple App Store for iOS devices and the Google Play Store for Android devices.
  • Truth+ connected TV apps are available in the relevant app stores for Roku, Apple, Android, and Amazon Fire TVs.
  • DJT -2.58% premarket to $16.23
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Simon Property Group upgraded at Stifel, citing shares are undervalued
data.description:
Retail REIT Simon Property Group (NYSE:SPG) was upgraded at the financial services firm Stifel, citing the shares have pulled back and are undervalued.

Shares have lost ~18.5% year-to-date, and are trading ~13% below its 20-day simple moving average. The stock was down 0.49% Wednesday pre-market to $139.68, and reached its 52-week low of $138.11 on Monday, April 7.

Stifel upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold, but reduced the target price to $168.50 from $178.00.

"Our $168.50 target price reflects a 13.0x multiple on 2026E FFO and a 6.25% implied cap rate," said Stifel analysts.

"We believe that with a cleaner story and more earnings contribution from core real estate FFO, there is room for multiple expansion here," said the research note.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system sees the stock as Hold, wthe a grade of C for Growth and C+ for Momentum.

data.title: Gilat Satellite Networks receives $11 million defense contract
data.description:
• Gilat Satellite Networks (NASDAQ:GILT) announced on Wednesday, Gilat Defense was awarded a contract exceeding $11 million for DKET 3420 terminals.
  • "These portable satellite communication hubs are designed to deliver robust connectivity, scalability, and flexibility for mission-critical operations," said the company, adding that, "deliveries are scheduled for later in the year."
  • Shares of GILT rose +2.74% to $5.62 in premarket trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Nevada King Gold upsizes private placement to $11.5 million
data.description:
• Nevada King Gold (TSXV:NKG:CA) on Wednesday announced an increase to its $11 million non-brokered private placement previously announced on April 7, 2025.
  • Due to demand from existing shareholders, the company said, it intends to raise gross proceeds of $11.5 million through the issuance of 46 million common shares priced at $0.25 per share.
  • The net proceeds of the offering are intended to be used to advance Nevada King's Atlanta Gold Mine Project and for other general working capital purposes. 
  • In connection with the offering, Collin Kettell, the company's chief executive officer, intends to subscribe for 22 million common shares.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: First Majestic reports record Q1 silver production
data.description:
• First Majestic Silver’s (NYSE:AG) Q1 production from the four producing underground mines in Mexico reached 7.7 million silver equivalent ounces, consisting of 3.7 million silver ounces, 36,469 gold ounces, 12.5 million pounds of zinc and 7.5 million pounds of lead.
  • First Majestic Silver (NYSE:AG) produced 3.7 million silver ounces in Q1 2025 representing an 88% increase when compared to 2.0 million silver ounces produced in Q1 2024.
  • The replacement of the diesel generators used for on-site back-up power at San Dimas with LNG units is near completion, with four 1 megawatt ("MW") LNG generators on site that will commence operating in Q2 2025.
  • The company expects this shift to LNG to generate substantial cost-savings and will result in a reduction in carbon emissions of up to 25%, when back-up power is required.

data.title: Teledyne FLIR Defense awarded $74.2M contract for U.S. Army
data.description:
• Teledyne FLIR Defense, part of Teledyne Technologies (NYSE:TDY), announced it has been awarded a four year, $74.2 million contract by the U.S. Army for continued development of the next-generation mounted reconnaissance capability for the M1135 Stryker’s Nuclear, Biological and Chemical Reconnaissance Vehicle Sensor Suite Upgrade Program.
  • TDY -0.52% premarket to $433.24.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Post Holdings announces the closure of two plants as cereal demand continues to decline
data.description:
Post Holdings (NYSE:POST) disclosed plans to close two of its Post Consumer Brands cereal manufacturing facilities.

The closures are for facilities in Cobourg, Ontario and Sparks, Nevada. The Cobourg facility has been part of the Post Consumer Brands business since July 2017 when Post (NYSE:POST) acquired Weetabix. The Sparks facility has been part of the business since June 2021, when Post (POST) acquired the Treehouse Foods ready-to-eat cereal business.

The company said the planned closure of the facilities reflects the need to reduce capacity in its cereal production network. The two facilities together employ approximately 300 employees and are expected to close by the end of December. Post Consumer Brands has notified employees of the decision. Production capabilities at the facilities will be transferred to other Post Consumer Brands manufacturing locations.

Post Consumer Brands CEO Nicolas Catoggio highlighted that the ready-to-eat cereal category continues to decline. "To respond to this, we are reducing excess manufacturing capacity and optimizing our North American plant network to better utilize our production capacity," he noted.

In terms of financials, Post (POST) currently expects to incur cash and noncash pre-tax charges totaling approximately $63.5 to $67.5 million in connection with the transfer of production capabilities to other Post Consumer Brands locations and closure of the facilities. Completion of the transfer and start-up of production at other locations is estimated to require capital expenditures of approximately $5 million to $7 million, incremental to the company's previously announced FY25 capital expenditures' guidance range of $380 to $420 million.

 As a result of the transfer of production capabilities to other locations and closure of the facilities, Post (POST) expects to achieve annual cost savings of approximately $21 to $23 million, starting in fiscal year 2026.

data.title: Ancora ends campaign, citing Trump's new CFIUS review of US Steel sale to Nippon Steel
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) +1.1% pre-market Wednesday after activist investor Ancora Holdings said it would withdraw its director nominations for the company's upcoming annual meeting, following President Trump's initiation of a new CFIUS review of the sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF).

Recent developments suggest US Steel (NYSE:X) and Nippon (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) have held productive conversations with the Trump administration, including discussions about increased capital commitments and addressing national security concerns.

Trump ordered a national security review of the planned sale earlier this week "to assist me in determining whether further action in this matter may be appropriate."

Ancora had detailed a plan for US Steel (X) shares to reach $75 without a Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF) deal, and has been pushing to replace US Steel's board and CEO David Burritt.

data.title: Apple slide extends as China hits back on tariffs, raises rate on US goods to 84%
data.description:
The slide in Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) shares continued Wednesday morning after China said it would ratchet up the tariff rate on U.S. goods to 84%.

“On April 8, 2025, the US government announced that it would increase the 'reciprocal tariff' on Chinese goods exported to the US from 34% to 84%,” the Chinese government said in a translated statement. “The US's practice of escalating tariffs on China is a mistake on top of a mistake, which seriously infringes on China's legitimate rights and interests and seriously damages the rules-based multilateral trading system.”

As such, China said it would raise the tariff rate on U.S. goods to 84%, effective 12:01 a.m. on April 10.

Shares fell 1.6% in premarket trading and have lost more than 23% of their value over the past four trading sessions. As a result, the iPhone maker has ceded its crown as the most valuable company in the U.S. to Microsoft (MSFT).

Despite efforts to diversify its supply chain geographically, Apple still relies on China for a significant portion of its manufacturing needs, specifically iPhones. Investment firm Needham recently estimated that Apple's fiscal 2025 earnings could be negatively impacted by 28% or more in a trade war between China and the U.S. unless it was given an exemption.

Aside from China, Apple and its partners manufacture iPhones, AirPods, Apple Watches, Macs and iPads in several countries, including India, Vietnam and Brazil. All of these countries have also been hit with tariffs by President Trump.

White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Tuesday that President Trump believes iPhones can be built in the U.S.

Apple releases its second quarter fiscal 2025 financial results on May 1. A consensus estimate expects Apple to report earnings per share of $1.61 on revenue of $94.04B.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.
